name: Schwarzwalder Kirschtorte.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 l Whipped Cream with a pinch of sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 26 cm spring form pan.
2. Preheat the oven to 180°C.
3. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Stir in the vanilla extract.
4. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Mix in the grated almonds and bitter chocolate until just combined.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, corn starch, and baking powder.
6. Gradually fold the dry ingredients into the butter mixture until smooth.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 30-40 minutes or until set in the center when tested with a skewer.
8. Allow the cake to cool completely at room temperature.
9. Once cooled, turn out onto a wire rack and let rest for at least 2 hours before slicing.
10. Cut the cake into three layers using a sharp knife.
11. Spread whipped cream on two of the lower layers, leaving about 2 cm bare in the center.
12. Arrange the fresh sour cherries over the cream layer.
13. Place the top cherry layer on top and spread remaining whipped cream with chocolate shavings over the very top.
14. Create rosettes from additional whipped cream and place a cherry atop each one before serving.
This recipe requires careful attention to detail but is well worth the effort for its rich flavor and beautiful presentation.
